summary: Broad signature-correction pass across 63 files, driven by an asm type-inference script described by the author as noisy but useful. The PR removed false parameters, corrected return types, tightened callback and pointer types, synchronized headers with implementations, and adjusted call sites. The decomp.dev report showed +292 matched bytes, 2 new full matches in gm_1601, and 11 other matching improvements.

key_files.reason: Changed Camera_8002E158 and Camera_8002E234 from s32 to bool and propagated boolean locals/returns.
Header prototypes updated to match camera bool return fixes.
Removed unused third parameter from Fighter_8006CDA4, used bool literals, and relied on PAD_STACK for stack shape.
Prototype synchronized for Fighter_8006CDA4(Fighter*, s32).
Updated Link/Samus tether calls after it_802A78B8 and it_802BAAE4 lost unused f32 parameters.
Updated tether call sites and replaced volatile dummy padding with PAD_STACK.
Typed gm_801674C4/fn_80169434 callback as bool (*)(s32), made fn_80168A6C void, and changed fn_80169444 to accept bool.
Changed lbl_8046B488.x1B8 from int (*)(void) to bool (*)(s32).
Made fn_80192E6C and fn_801967E0 void where asm did not require returned values.
Added Ground_GObj* parameters for grIceMt_801F96E0/grIceMt_801F9ACC and changed grIceMt_801FA0BC argument to s16*.
Largest edited file; mostly formatting plus grShrineRoute_8020AD24 argument changed from bool to s32.
Changed itColl_BounceOffVictim to void and it_80274EE8 to void(void), removing misleading returns/arguments.
Removed unused f32 parameter from it_802A78B8.
Removed unused f32 parameter from it_802BAAE4.
Changed it_802EAF34 from void to Item_GObj* and returned the spawned item object.
Changed it_802996D0 from void to Item_GObj* and returned the spawned projectile object.
Changed it_802C519C from void to Item_GObj* and returned the created shadowball object.
Typed lb_800138CC second parameter as HSD_GObjEvent and included baselib/psstructs.h.
Changed struct lb_800138D8_t.x18 from int to HSD_GObjEvent.
Moved/used CardState typedef earlier and changed fn_803AC258/fn_803B1338 prototypes to CardState*.

what_changed: Corrected many prototypes and definitions where asm implied a different argument count, return type, callback type, or pointer type than the current C.

Updated call sites to match removed dummy parameters, including Fighter_8006CDA4(fp, percent), ftKb_SpecialN_800F5898(thrower_gobj), ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttr28(), it_802A78B8(gobj, &pos), and it_802BAAE4(gobj, &pos).
Changed predicate-style integer returns to bool in camera functions: Camera_8002E158 and Camera_8002E234.

Changed several functions from non-void to void when their apparent return value was bogus or unused by the original ABI, including fn_80168A6C, fn_80192E6C, fn_801967E0, itColl_BounceOffVictim, it_802B1FC8, it_802B22B8, it_8028D390, it_802B64FC, it_802B6A60, it_802BBC38, and it_80291CCC.
Changed several item spawn helpers from void to returning Item_GObj*, including it_802EAF34, it_802996D0, and it_802C519C.
Corrected argument order for Ness yoyo creation: it_802BE9D8(gobj, fp->facing_dir, &sp10, fp->motion_id).
Typed gm_1601 callback plumbing more precisely: gm_801674C4 and fn_80169434 now use bool (*)(s32), fn_80169444 accepts bool, and lbl_8046B488.x1B8 stores bool (*)(s32).
Typed library/baselib callbacks and state pointers more precisely: lb_800138CC takes HSD_GObjEvent, lb_800138D8_t.x18 is HSD_GObjEvent, fn_803AC258 and fn_803B1338 use CardState*.

Used PAD_STACK in places where removing dummy arguments or temporary variables still needed the stack layout to match, including ftCo_ThrownMewtwo, Fighter_8006CDA4, ftCo_Attack100, and item functions.
Kept many edits as header/implementation synchronization and low-risk formatting/cast cleanup rather than committing the noisy inference scripts.
smart_moves: The author used asm-derived type inference as a broad audit tool, then manually filtered noisy output instead of committing the tool or trusting it wholesale.
Signature changes were propagated through definitions, headers, struct fields, and call sites in the same PR, reducing risk of stale prototypes.
Returning Item_GObj* from item spawn helpers preserved the object already constructed in the function instead of treating a real return register as void.
Removing fake trailing parameters simplified call sites while PAD_STACK preserved required stack allocation where matching still depended on it.
Replacing s32/int with bool or HSD_GObjEvent made the code express ABI behavior and callback intent more directly without changing symbol names.
The gm_1601 callback fix did more than change a prototype: it corrected the storage field type and the callback invocation to pass the bool/s32 argument.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiler emits extra unused parameters, verify against register use in asm; fake parameters can harm both readability and matching.
Do not assume a function returning a nonzero expression in C truly returns a value in the original; several functions matched better as void.
Conversely, item spawn functions that create and keep a gobj may need to return Item_GObj* even when earlier decomp code used void.
Boolean predicates should use bool in both prototypes and locals when the asm is storing/combining 0/1 results.
Callback fields must be typed at the storage site as well as at setter/caller functions; otherwise casts and M2C_FIELD workarounds hide real ABI evidence.
Global/ext-attribute getter functions may not need a gobj parameter if the implementation uses global data, as with ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttr28(void).
Use project padding macros like PAD_STACK instead of dummy volatile arrays or fake parameters when the issue is stack shape rather than semantics.
Header fixes are part of matching work: mismatched declarations can force casts, wrong call order, or incorrect return handling across unrelated files.
Tool-assisted asm audits are useful for finding candidate mismatches, but the PR body explicitly notes false positives/negatives, so each finding still needs local validation.
naming_conventions: No broad symbol renaming occurred; the PR preserved existing address-based names such as Camera_8002E158, fn_80168A6C, and it_802EAF34.
Use typed object aliases in signatures when known: Fighter_GObj*, Item_GObj*, Ground_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, and CardState*.
Use bool for true/false return values and callback results instead of s32/int when the code treats values as booleans.
Use HSD_GObjEvent for gobj event callback storage/parameters instead of plain int.
Use void parameter lists for no-argument functions, e.g. ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttr28(void) and it_80274EE8(void).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Author described a Claude-written script that inferred types by examining asm; it was not committed because it was noisy and partly garbage after the cleanup.
Validate inferred signatures by checking whether arguments are actually consumed and whether return registers are meaningful at call sites.
Replace bogus return variables with void returns when asm/control flow does not require a returned value.
Use PAD_STACK to preserve stack size after removing fake locals, fake args, or old workaround branches.
For callbacks, infer both the function pointer type and the callback invocation signature from asm, as shown by lbl_8046B488.x1B8 and fn_80169444.
For pointer-vs-integer mismatches, prefer the real struct pointer type when downstream calls immediately cast/use it as that pointer, as with CardState*.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 63.10%, +0.01%, +292 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported 2 new matches: main/melee/gm/gm_1601 fn_80168A6C and fn_80169444 reached 100%.

decomp.dev bot reported 11 improvements in unmatched items, including ftCo_8008DCE0, it_802BBC38, grIceMt_801F7728, it_802B64FC, ftCo_800BDB58, ftCo_800BE000, it_802996D0, it_802C519C, it_802EAF34, it_802B22B8, and it_802DC4BC.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining prototypes with unused trailing parameters that are not consumed in asm.
Audit item spawn helpers currently declared void to see whether they actually return Item_GObj*.
Search for callback fields still typed int, s32, or void* where call sites imply HSD_GObjEvent or bool (*)(s32).
Check other global/ext-attribute getters for unnecessary gobj parameters.
Review remaining s32 predicate returns in camera, ground, and item code for possible bool conversion.
Look for fake return values in unmatched functions where decompiler output returns a local or pointer only to satisfy a guessed signature.
